question.text: What is a Consolidated Account Statement (CAS)?

answer.text: A statement showing only the holdings of one particular fund house
A single combined statement showing an investor's mutual fund and demat holdings across all fund houses
A bank account statement showing SIP deductions
A tax computation statement from the Income Tax department

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CAS (Consolidated Account Statement) is a single combined statement that shows all mutual fund investments of an investor across all fund houses, linked to a single PAN. It is generated by CAMS and KFintech (the two main RTAs) and sent to the investor's email. CAS shows holdings, transactions, and NAV values. **The Trap:** CAS covers ALL fund houses, not just one. It is generated based on PAN number. Monthly CAS is sent only if there are transactions in that month. Half-yearly CAS is sent to all investors even without transactions. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** CAS is very useful for retired investors to get a complete view of their mutual fund portfolio in one place. Help them register their email ID with all fund houses to receive CAS regularly. They can also check CAS through MFCentral or CAMS/KFintech websites.
